persona.name: Jordan
persona.description: UX researcher with survey design experience. Understands data types and form flow but not database schemas. Has used Typeform, SurveyMonkey, and Qualtrics extensively.

initialIntent:
## Purpose
Collect intake information from new fitness coaching clients including their health history, fitness goals, and availability.

## Audience
New clients signing up for personal training or group fitness coaching.

## Exclusions
Do not collect detailed medical records — only relevant health conditions that affect exercise safety.
finalIntent: {"purpose":{"content":"Collect intake information from new fitness coaching clients including their health history, fitness goals, and availability.","updatedAt":"2026-03-24T23:46:15.767Z"},"audience":{"content":"New clients signing up for personal training or group fitness coaching.","updatedAt":"2026-03-24T23:46:15.767Z"},"exclusions":{"content":"Do not collect detailed medical records — only relevant health conditions that affect exercise safety.","updatedAt":"2026-03-24T23:46:15.767Z"},"constraints":{"content":"","updatedAt":"2026-03-24T23:46:15.768Z"}}

opinions.text: Should we include a section for clients to specify their preferred training intensity level (e.g., beginner, intermediate, advanced)?
Would it be beneficial to add a field for clients to indicate any specific injuries or limitations that may affect their training?
Should we allow clients to select multiple preferred training types (e.g., strength training, cardio, flexibility) instead of just one?
Is it necessary to include a field for clients to share their motivation for seeking fitness coaching?
Should we add a question about the best times for clients to be contacted regarding their training schedule?

schemaDescription: 10 fields: Full Name, Email Address, Phone Number, Emergency Contact Name, Emergency Contact Phone Number, Relevant Health Conditions, Fitness Goals, Prior Exercise Experience, Availability for Training, Preferred Training Type
provenanceSummary: Schema generated once. 5 opinion resolutions recorded. 6 total provenance entries.
